Title: Jasmonic acid plays an important role in mediating retrograde signaling under mitochondrial translational stress to balance plant growth and defense

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: balances plant growth and defense; promotes biotic stress resistance under mitochondrial translational stress
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of MRPL1 increases JA accumulation, reduces plant growth, and enhances resistance to Spodoptera exigua and Botrytis cinerea
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. mrpl1-3 mutants showed elevated JA content and increased expression of JA biosynthetic genes. 2. mrpl1-3 displayed reduced root growth and shorter root meristems, which were partially rescued by coi1-2 or erf109 mutation. 3. MRPL1-dependent mitochondrial translational stress activated JA-mediated defense responses and increased resistance to insect feeding and necrotrophic pathogen infection.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: loss-of-function mutant analysis (mrpl1-3); JA quantification by LC-MS; RT-qPCR of JA biosynthetic genes; genetic crosses with coi1-2 and erf109; Jas9-Venus reporter; insect feeding assay; Botrytis infection assay
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Modulating MRPL1 or mitochondrial translation status could tune JA-mediated growth–defense tradeoffs and enhance biotic stress resistance, though growth penalties are likely
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced growth, shorter roots, developmental retardation
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: MRPL1 is not a biosynthetic enzyme or TF for JA; it acts as an upstream mitochondrial translational component triggering retrograde JA signaling.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: mediates growth inhibition and defense signaling during mitochondrial translational stress
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: COI1 is required for JA-mediated inhibition of root and shoot growth under mitochondrial translational stress and contributes to UPRmt/retrograde marker induction
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. coi1-2 and coi1-1 mutants were less sensitive to Dox-induced root and stem growth inhibition. 2. coi1-2 partially rescued mrpl1-3 growth defects and attenuated anthocyanin accumulation. 3. Dox-induced ERF109 and retrograde marker genes mtHSC70-1, mtHSC70-5, and AOX1a were reduced in coi1-2; H2O2-induced ERF109 expression also required COI1.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: loss-of-function mutant analysis (coi1-1, coi1-2); reporter imaging with pCOI1:COI1-Venus; genetic cross with mrpl1-3; RT-qPCR of target genes; OCR measurement; root/shoot phenotyping
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: doxycycline; antimycin A; hydrogen peroxide; DIECA
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: COI1-Venus signal increased in root tips after Dox treatment
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: F-box jasmonate receptor; SCFCOI1 complex component
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Targeting COI1-dependent signaling could reduce growth penalties from mitochondrial stress, but may compromise defense outputs
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced JA signaling alleviates growth inhibition but may weaken defense and retrograde stress adaptation
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Classified as Common Gene because it is a hormone receptor/signaling component rather than pathway enzyme or TF.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts fatty acid hydroperoxide intermediates into allene oxides in the jasmonate biosynthetic pathway
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for synthesis of jasmonic acid precursor pool
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control step not established in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: stress hormone coordinating growth inhibition and defense
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: AOS expression is induced by mitochondrial translational stress and elevated in mrpl1-3, supporting increased JA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Dox induced AOSpro:GUS activity and AOS transcript accumulation. 2. AOS was upregulated in mrpl1-3 mutants. 3. Dox-induced AOS expression was attenuated in myc2 and myc234, linking JA biosynthesis to MYC-dependent signaling.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: AOSpro:GUS reporter; RT-qPCR; mutant comparison; Dox treatment
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in seedlings upon short-term Dox treatment
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulating AOS could adjust JA production under organellar stress, but elevated JA may suppress growth
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher JA biosynthesis is associated with root growth inhibition and reduced biomass
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: AOS was monitored as a major representative JA biosynthetic gene in this study.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: coordinates growth inhibition and defense under mitochondrial translational stress
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates ERF109 directly by binding G-box-like motifs in its promoter; positively promotes induction of JA biosynthesis genes under Dox stress
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: positive feedback on JA biosynthesis is supported by reduced AOS/LOX2/LOX3 induction in myc2 and myc234
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: COI1-dependent jasmonate signaling; mitochondrial translational stress induced by doxycycline
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: functionally antagonized by JAZ1; part of JA signaling module downstream of SCFCOI1
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: major JA-responsive regulator mediating Dox-induced growth inhibition and ERF109 activation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: MYC2 negatively regulates plant growth under mitochondrial translational stress by promoting JA biosynthesis gene induction and activating ERF109
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. myc2 and myc234 mutants were more tolerant to Dox, showing longer primary roots, greater shoot fresh weight, and less stem inhibition than Col-0. 2. Dox-induced AOS, LOX2, and LOX3 expression was attenuated in myc2 and myc234. 3. MYC2 directly bound the ERF109 promoter in ChIP-qPCR and Y1H assays and activated ERF109 promoter activity in transient assays; JAZ1 repressed this activation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: loss-of-function mutant analysis (myc2, myc234); RT-qPCR; ChIP-qPCR; yeast one-hybrid; transient dual-luciferase assay in Nicotiana benthamiana; phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: functional activity inferred under Dox treatment; direct expression pattern not specifically reported
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Tuning MYC2 activity may alleviate growth penalties under mitochondrial stress or enhance defense, but strong activation can intensify JA-related growth repression
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced MYC2 improves growth under stress but may impair defense-associated JA outputs
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: MYC3 and MYC4 function redundantly with MYC2, but MYC2 received direct mechanistic validation for ERF109 promoter binding.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: mediates JA-linked root growth inhibition and stress adaptation via ROS feedback
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: retrograde stress transcription and ROS-associated growth regulation downstream of JA signaling
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation downstream of MYC2 and COI1; promotes H2O2 accumulation forming positive feedback
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: ROS-associated outputs; exact direct metabolic targets not defined in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: acts downstream of MYC2 to mediate stress-induced transcription and promote H2O2 production
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: positive feedback with H2O2; H2O2 induces ERF109, and ERF109 overexpression increases H2O2 accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: MYC2/MYC3/MYC4; COI1-dependent jasmonate signaling; ethylene signaling components ETR2, EIN2, EIN3, EIN4; H2O2
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: essential downstream node linking JA signaling to H2O2-mediated growth repression under mitochondrial translational stress
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ERF109 is required for full Dox-induced inhibition of root and shoot growth and contributes to mrpl1-3 growth defects by promoting H2O2 accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ERF109 was rapidly and transiently induced by Dox and this induction required COI1 and MYC2/3/4, as well as ethylene signaling components. 2. erf109 mutants were less sensitive to Dox and partially rescued mrpl1-3 root defects, whereas 35S-ERF109 plants had reduced growth even without stress. 3. Dox-induced ERF109 expression in roots was reduced in myc2 background, and ERF109 formed a positive amplification loop with H2O2.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: loss-of-function mutant and overexpression analysis; RT-qPCR time course; promoter-GUS reporter; genetic cross with mrpl1-3 and myc2; DAB staining; KI scavenger treatment
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: doxycycline; hydrogen peroxide; potassium iodide; mitochondrial translational stress
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: rapid transient induction in seedlings and root tips after Dox; peaks at 20 min and returns near basal by 2 h
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: ERF109 is a candidate lever for balancing growth repression and defense under organellar stress, but overexpression causes constitutive stress and growth defects
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression reduces growth and elevates ROS; loss reduces stress-induced growth inhibition
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although ERF109 is not a JA biosynthetic regulator per se, it is a decisive downstream TF linking JA signaling to ROS-mediated developmental outcomes.
